release_review.summary: Workbench release: integrates layered canvas objects, sticky notes, annotations, background regions, floe-webapp 0.36.45, runtime-backed layout persistence, projected surface performance work, and scoped render transactions for theme and mode changes. No Runtime Service protocol or compatibility window changes.
release_review.same_window_rationale: The release changes Env App Workbench UI, runtime-owned Workbench layout persistence, and Desktop shell rendering coordination, but it does not add or change Runtime Service snapshot fields, sys.ping payload semantics, attach/start contracts, startup reports, or SSH bootstrap requirements. Older runtimes continue serving their own Env App bundle and do not need the new layered Workbench APIs from this release, so the existing protocol version, compatibility epoch, minimum Desktop version, and minimum Runtime version remain valid.
checked_surfaces: Env App Workbench layered canvas modes and dock routing
Env App Workbench sticky notes, text annotations, and background region persistence
Runtime Workbench layout schema migration and snapshot/event APIs
Workbench projected surface drag, resize, pan, zoom, and theme/mode render transactions
Desktop embedded drag regions during Workbench theme changes
Desktop managed runtime start, attach, and open gating
startup report and local-ui runtime state carriers
Env App sys.ping runtime service payload
Env App desktop first-screen readiness IPC
SSH-managed runtime bootstrap and attach flow
